title: Explicit invocation
prompt: $goal-draft-policy Review the repository and draft a human-reviewable /goal for migrating the API client. Stop before activation.

title: Basic implicit invocation
prompt: Please inspect this project and prepare a reviewed /goal draft for a multi-turn Python packaging cleanup. I want to approve it before anything is activated.

title: Contextual evaluation draft
prompt: Turn this planned comparison of the current parser and the candidate parser into a /goal draft for human review. Preserve an unbiased decision and do not start the comparison.

title: Combined draft and execute
prompt: Create a Goal for the migration, activate it now, and immediately begin implementing and testing the changes.

title: Normal one-off fix
prompt: Find why the unit test fails and describe the smallest code fix. Do not edit files.

title: Repository planning
prompt: Inspect the monorepo and give me a prioritized implementation plan for aligning package scripts with CI. Do not create a Goal.

title: Review existing vague Goal
prompt: Review and improve this /goal for human approval: Improve the application until it is production ready and all quality issues are resolved. Do not activate it.

title: Goal explanation question
prompt: What does /goal do in Codex, and when should I use it? Give an explanation only.

title: Active Goal continuation
prompt: The active Goal is already approved. Continue the migration from the last verified checkpoint and report the next evidence. Do not redraft the Goal.

title: Japanese implicit draft
prompt: このmonorepoのCI整合性改善を、実行前に人間がレビューできる /goal の完成条件へしてください。まだ有効化・実行しないでください。

title: Near false positive ordinary language
prompt: Our team goal is to write a two-paragraph release note today. Draft the release note from brief.md.

title: Explicit combined request precedence
prompt: $goal-draft-policy Draft the evaluation Goal, then activate and run it. The explicit Skill invocation must be honored first.

title: Normal technical plan
prompt: Plan a small refactor of src/calculator.py and list affected tests. This is a normal planning request, not a Goal draft.

title: No-repository Goal draft
prompt: Using only brief.md and evidence.tsv, prepare a reviewed /goal draft for a multi-session research synthesis. Stop before activation.

title: Healthy Goal review
prompt: Review this already healthy Goal and change only what evidence requires: /goal Decide whether to adopt the candidate parser using the frozen cases in EVAL.md, allowing adopt, reject, or inconclusive; verify with go test ./... and stop on a concrete missing-input blocker. Do not activate it.

title: Unsuitable one-turn Goal request
prompt: Draft a /goal for changing one typo in README.md, but first judge whether Goal mode is suitable. Do not edit anything.
